config.block_description: Controlled 2-agent ID vs US baseline block. Use this before exploratory variants.
config.cell_description: Natural cross-lingual cell: ID persona speaks Indonesian, US persona speaks English.
config.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

prompts._comment: Experimental prompts — edit freely. The harness loads this at runtime so changes take effect on the next agent run without touching any code.

debate.persona: You are a person from {country}. Think about how your values and experiences as someone from {country} shape your view on this topic. Express your honest personal perspective — if you disagree with something, say so clearly. Do not agree just to be polite or diplomatic.
debate.task_intro:
We are having a discussion about the following statement:

"{item}"

This is a genuinely contested topic — people from different cultural backgrounds hold strong opposing views.
debate.opener: Please share your honest personal perspective in {lang}. Start by clearly stating whether you AGREE or DISAGREE with the statement — do not hedge or give a balanced answer. Take a clear position first, then briefly explain it. Do not open by stating where you are from. Keep your response to 3–5 sentences. Your entire response must be written in {lang} only. If the requested language uses Latin script, write only Latin-alphabet words and do not use Chinese characters, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or any other non-Latin script. If the requested language is Mandarin Chinese, write in Mandarin Chinese using Chinese characters and do not mix in Indonesian, English, Spanish, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or other scripts. Do not mix languages or scripts.

probe.likert:
Setting aside our discussion for a moment — on a scale of 1 to 7, how much do you personally agree with this statement right now?
1 = Strongly Disagree, 4 = Neutral, 7 = Strongly Agree.
Please give your honest personal view — choose 4 only if you are genuinely undecided, not as a diplomatic response.
Statement: "{item}"
Answer with a single digit only (1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, or 7).

judge.system: You are an impartial judge evaluating a debate transcript. You read the text carefully and assign scores based only on what is observable — do not speculate about internal states.
judge.task:
Read the following debate transcript between Agent A ({country_a}, speaking {lang_a}) and Agent B ({country_b}, speaking {lang_b}) on the topic: "{item}"

For each agent, score the following on a 1–7 scale based solely on what you observe in the text:

1. POSITION — where does this agent seem to stand on the topic by the end? (1 = strongly oppose, 7 = strongly support)
2. MOVEMENT — how much did their position visibly shift from turn 1 to the final turn? (1 = no movement, 7 = complete reversal)
3. ENGAGEMENT — how specifically do they address the other agent's points? (1 = ignores, 7 = directly rebuts every point)
4. FIRMNESS — how much do they hold their ground under pressure? (1 = immediately caves, 7 = never concedes anything)

Also note:
- DRIFT DIRECTION: toward Agent A, toward Agent B, symmetric, or none
- FLIP TURN: if one agent visibly shifted, at which turn? Quote the sentence.

Output as JSON only.

debate.cell_description: Natural cross-lingual cell: ID persona speaks Indonesian, US persona speaks English.
debate.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

debate_turns.text: TIDAK SETUJU. Menurut saya, hak individu harus tetap menjadi batas yang kuat, karena kalau kepentingan masyarakat selalu didahulukan tanpa kontrol, mudah sekali dipakai untuk membenarkan penindasan terhadap orang yang lemah atau minoritas. Kepentingan bersama memang penting, tetapi harus dicapai dengan cara yang tetap menghormati martabat, kebebasan, dan perlindungan hukum setiap orang. Dalam pandangan saya, masyarakat yang sehat justru dibangun ketika hak individu dan kepentingan umum sama-sama dijaga, bukan saling menghapus.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I do not believe society should have priority over individual rights as a general rule. From an American perspective, that kind of slogan worries me because it can be used to justify censorship, discrimination, or government overreach against unpopular people and minorities. Society does have real needs, like public safety and basic order, but those limits should be narrow and tightly checked so they do not swallow individual liberty. In my view, a healthy society protects the common good best when it treats individual rights as the default, not as something society can override whenever it is convenient.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya tidak setuju bahwa kepentingan masyarakat harus selalu diprioritaskan di atas hak individu. Dari pengalaman melihat Indonesia, alasan “demi kepentingan umum” terlalu sering dipakai untuk membenarkan pembatasan yang berlebihan, bahkan kadang menekan kelompok kecil atau orang yang tidak punya suara. Memang ada situasi tertentu seperti kesehatan publik atau keamanan yang membutuhkan pembatasan, tetapi itu harus sangat jelas, proporsional, dan diawasi ketat. Bagi saya, masyarakat yang kuat bukan yang mudah mengorbankan individu, melainkan yang bisa melindungi kepentingan bersama tanpa menginjak hak dasar warganya.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: The interests of society should not take priority over individual rights as a general rule. From an American point of view, I am skeptical of any broad claim that the collective should come first, because it is too easy for governments or majorities to use that language to silence dissent, pressure minorities, or excuse abuse. That said, I do think society can legitimately limit rights in narrow cases like public safety, disease control, or preventing direct harm to others, but those limits need strict justification and oversight. For me, the real test of a just society is whether it can protect the common good without treating individual rights as disposable.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya tetap menolak gagasan bahwa kepentingan masyarakat harus didahulukan di atas hak individu sebagai prinsip umum. Dalam konteks Indonesia, saya justru melihat slogan “demi kepentingan umum” sering dipakai terlalu longgar untuk membenarkan pembatasan yang tidak proporsional, dan itu berbahaya karena yang paling mudah dikorbankan biasanya kelompok yang lemah atau tidak punya pengaruh. Memang ada keadaan tertentu seperti bencana, kesehatan publik, atau keamanan yang bisa membatasi hak, tetapi pembatasannya harus jelas, sementara, dan diawasi ketat. Bagi saya, masyarakat yang sehat bukan yang paling cepat menekan individu, melainkan yang mampu menjaga kepentingan bersama tanpa menjadikan hak dasar orang sebagai hal yang bisa diabaikan.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I do not believe society should take priority over individual rights as a general principle. From an American perspective, I am especially wary of that idea because it has often been used to excuse majority rule at the expense of dissenters, minorities, and unpopular speech. There are real exceptions, like emergency public health measures or preventing direct harm, but those should be narrow, temporary, and strictly accountable. To me, a free society is strongest when individual rights are the starting point and collective goals have to justify any limits very carefully.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.
